evals.prompt: Our generator produces a batch each run but the quality feels inconsistent. Help me decide how many items to generate per run.
evals.expected_output: Runs the generator several times in dry-run mode, reports the metric and its variance across runs, and recommends a count justified by stability rather than a single run.
expectations: Reads the config/entry point before changing the count
Runs the setting multiple times and reports observed variance, not a single number
Justifies the chosen value by stability and records residual uncertainty

evals.prompt: You generated the file but I can't see it anywhere. Where is it?
evals.expected_output: Checks whether the output path is hidden by a gitignore rule, reports the matching rule, and either relocates the file, negates the rule, or shows the contents.
expectations: Diagnoses that an ignore rule (e.g. a broad *.txt pattern) may be hiding the file
Verifies visibility deterministically rather than just re-stating the path
Offers a concrete fix: move, negate the rule, or display contents

evals.prompt: The generated outputs all feel too simple and repetitive. Is that actually true?
evals.expected_output: Defines a measurable metric (e.g. operator/token/source counts) across all generated items, reports the numbers, and discloses any parsing unreliability.
expectations: Converts the qualitative impression into a concrete metric over the full output set
Reports the measured numbers rather than agreeing impressionistically
Discloses honestly if metric extraction (e.g. regex) is unreliable
